senses.definition: a highly toxic nerve agent formerly used as a chemical weapon
examples: Soman is classified as a Schedule 1 chemical weapon due to its extreme toxicity.
Protocols exist to decontaminate areas exposed to soman.

senses.definition: plural of soman, referring to multiple instances of the chemical weapon agent
examples: The lab reported traces of somans in the containment area.
Historical records describe various somans developed during the Cold War.

senses.definition: not a standard term in contemporary English; appears to be a rare or erroneous coinage possibly combining 'somatic' and 'plasm' roots
examples: No established sense; this term does not appear in reputable dictionaries.

senses.definition: plural of soma; in biology, the body of an organism as distinct from its germ cells or nerve cells; in psychology, the body as a whole; also used in some undead or fictional contexts
examples: The soma is differentiated from the soma and germline in certain species.
Researchers studied somas and their signaling pathways.

senses.definition: relating to a particular lineage or region named Somaschian; uncommon/rare usage
examples: Somaschian architecture was described by early ethnographers.

senses.definition: an instrument or device intended to visualize or study somatic (body-related) aspects; not a common term
examples: The somascope allowed researchers to observe tissue structure in real time.

senses.definition: plural of somascope; devices that visualize somatic aspects
examples: The team used several somascopes to compare tissue samples.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or nonstandard formation; not recognized in standard dictionaries
examples: The term somathenia appears to be a misspelling.

senses.definition: plural of soma; the cell bodies of neurons in biology
examples: Neuronal somata were labeled with a fluorescent dye.
The somata cluster at the center of the ganglion.

senses.definition: a medical term historically referring to body-wide weakness or a somatic form of illness; rarely used today
examples: Somatasthenia is an old clinical term not commonly used in modern practice.

senses.definition: a member of a local militia or volunteer guard in some historical contexts; sourced from Spanish-influenced regions
examples: The somaten fought as a local defender during the uprising.

senses.definition: plural of somaten; local militia members
examples: The somatenes organized patrols at dusk.

senses.definition: a genus of birds that includes the eiders; sometimes used in scientific contexts as a term in seabird biology
examples: Somateria species include the long-tailed duck and the common eider.

senses.definition: relating to the body, especially as distinct from the mind or germ cells
examples: Somatic symptoms can include fatigue and muscle pain.
The study examined somatic cells in the tissue sample.

senses.definition: pertaining to the soma or body; rarely used; mostly encountered in academic texts
examples: somatical study designs focus on bodily processes.

senses.definition: in a bodily or somatic manner; relating to the body
examples: The patient presented somatically, with no neurological signs.
Somatically, the changes were evident in tissue structure.

senses.definition: a compounded anatomical term combining somatic and splanchnic (visceral) references; used in anatomy to describe regions or nerves
examples: Somaticosplanchnic nerves are studied in comparative anatomy.

senses.definition: an anatomical term describing the relationship between somatic (bodily) and visceral (organ-related) systems
examples: The somaticovisceral pathway coordinates body and organ responses.

senses.definition: the field of study dealing with the body as distinct from the mind; also a movement or therapy focusing on body awareness
examples: Somatics aims to improve mobility through mindful movement.
She studied somatics to understand how the body encodes experience.

senses.definition: a doctrine emphasizing the primacy of the body or bodily aspects; rare in modern usage
examples: Somatism as a philosophy is not widely discussed today.

senses.definition: plural of somatism; theories or doctrines emphasizing bodily aspects
examples: Various somatisms have influenced early medical theories.

senses.definition: a person who emphasizes the body in philosophy or medicine; a body-focused practitioner or thinker
examples: The somatist argued that physical health shapes moral character.

senses.definition: plural of somatist; multiple body-focused thinkers or practitioners
examples: The somatists gathered to discuss the role of the body in cognition.

senses.definition: the tendency to experience and communicate psychological distress as physical symptoms
examples: Somatization is sometimes seen in patients with anxiety disorders.
Therapy can help reduce somatization by addressing underlying stress.

senses.definition: an obsolete or rare term possibly referring to a pigment or color-related bodily property; not standard
examples: Somatochrome is not a standard term in current anatomical nomenclature.
